name: Deviled Swiss Steak
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the flour, salt, pepper, and dry mustard in a shallow dish.
2. Sprinkle the mixture evenly over the steak.
3. Pound the steak with a meat mallet until it is an even thickness.
4. Heat the cooking oil in a heavy skillet or Dutch oven.
5. Brown the steak in batches, turning occasionally, until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 2-3 minutes per side.
6. Remove the steak from the skillet and set aside.
7. Add the Worcestershire sauce to the skillet and deglaze, scraping up any browned bits.
8. Stir in the mushrooms and water.
9. Return the steak to the skillet with the mushroom mixture.
10. Cover tightly and transfer to a 325-degree oven.
11. Cook for 1 hour or until tender.
12. Heat the remaining mushrooms in a small amount of butter until crisp.
13. Serve the heated mushrooms on top of the cooked steak.
